name: Federal Work-Study Program
description: Provides part-time jobs for undergraduate and graduate students with financial need, allowing them to earn money to help pay education expenses. Jobs may be on-campus or off-campus with approved employers.
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students with financial need as determined by FAFSA. Must be enrolled at least half-time. Availability depends on college's Work-Study funding allocation.
award_amount: Varies by school and student need. Typical awards range from $1,500 to $5,000 per academic year. Students are paid at least federal minimum wage, often higher for specialized positions.
application_process: Indicate interest on FAFSA. College financial aid office determines eligibility and includes it in award letter. Student must find and secure a Work-Study job through the college's employment office.
timeline: Awarded per academic year. Jobs typically posted before semester starts. Earnings are paid biweekly and not applied directly to tuition bill unless student arranges it.
advisor_notes: Work-Study earnings are excluded from next year's FAFSA income, which is a significant advantage over regular employment. Encourage students to find jobs related to their major. Unused Work-Study awards cannot be carried over — use it or lose it.
